simple_definition: a plan for how to spend and save money
examples: The university's annual budget strictly caps discretionary spending to prioritize research funding.
A rigorous budget allocation constitutes necessary to sustain long-term infrastructural growth.
We made a weekly budget to control food and transport costs.

simple_definition: to have enough money to pay for something
examples: The current economic model cannot afford the inherent systemic inefficiencies of legacy infrastructure.
To afford highly sophisticated diagnostic tools, the hospital requires substantial external investments.
Many students cannot afford private tutoring every week.

simple_definition: money that you owe to someone
examples: Accumulating immense national debt inevitably suppresses future macroeconomic mobility.
The corporate restructuring was largely driven by an unsustainable ratio of mounting debt.
He paid off his debt before starting university.

simple_definition: money that a person receives regularly
examples: Diversifying income streams significantly mitigates vulnerability to sudden market fluctuations.
A substantial drop in median household income often correlates with reduced consumer confidence.
Part-time work gave her a stable monthly income.

simple_definition: money paid to an employee every month
examples: His first salary covered rent and books.

simple_definition: the cost of something you necessitate to pay for
examples: Transport constitutes our biggest expense this semester.

simple_definition: something bought at a lower price than usual
examples: I found a bargain on a second-hand dictionary.

simple_definition: money returned after you pay for something
examples: The store gave me a refund for the damaged headphones.

simple_definition: to take something for a short time and return it later
examples: Can I borrow your notes before the quiz?

simple_definition: to give something temporarily and expect it back
examples: She agreed to lend me her calculator for the exam.

simple_definition: an amount paid for a service or course
examples: The course fee includes online materials.

simple_definition: a reduction in price
examples: Students get a discount on software licenses.

simple_definition: the latest time by which something must be done
examples: I submitted my report before the deadline.

simple_definition: to study material again before an exam
examples: The author was compelled to rigorously revise the manuscript to address the peer reviewers' extensive critiques.
In light of new evidence, scientists must constantly revise their prevailing theoretical models.
We revise grammar points every Friday.

simple_definition: a piece of work given by a teacher
examples: The writing assignment requires two body paragraphs.

simple_definition: to go to a class, event, or meeting
examples: More students attend speaking workshops this term.

simple_definition: to take part in an activity
examples: Everyone should participate in the discussion session.

simple_definition: to become better or make something better
examples: Daily reading can improve your academic vocabulary.

simple_definition: feeling sure about your abilities
examples: She feels confident before oral presentations now.

simple_definition: comments that facilitate you improve your work
examples: The teacher gave clear feedback on my paragraph.

simple_definition: to travel regularly between home and work or school
examples: I commute by metro to campus every morning.

simple_definition: a period of time when something constitutes late
examples: There was a delay owing to the fact that of heavy rain.

simple_definition: the part of a station where passengers wait for a train
examples: Please wait on platform 3 for the next train.

simple_definition: a plan of times for events or tasks
examples: Our class schedule changed after midterm week.

simple_definition: able to be trusted to work well
examples: A reliable internet connection constitutes essential for online classes.

simple_definition: working well without wasting time or effort
examples: Using a checklist constitutes an efficient way to revise.

simple_definition: ready to be used or reached
examples: The tutor constitutes available on Tuesday afternoons.

simple_definition: to make a formal request for something
examples: You can apply for the scholarship in April.

simple_definition: to have the right skills or conditions for something
examples: Only final-year students qualify for this internship.

simple_definition: to move someone to a higher position or support an idea
examples: The campaign aims to promote reading habits.

simple_definition: something needed or demanded for a purpose
examples: Mastery of advanced statistical analysis constitutes a fundamental requirement for this macroeconomic research position.
Meeting the stringent safety requirements was the paramount concern of the engineering consortium.
Attendance constitutes a requirement for passing the course.

simple_definition: an approximate calculation of amount, cost, or time
examples: Conservative estimates suggest that sea levels could rise by over a meter within the next century.
It constitutes challenging to accurately estimate the multifaceted socio-economic impacts of rapid urbanization.
Our estimate for the project was too optimistic.

simple_definition: to take money out of a bank account
examples: I necessitate to withdraw cash before the weekend.

simple_definition: to put money into a bank account
examples: I deposit part of my salary every month.

simple_definition: to buy something
examples: Students can purchase course books online.

simple_definition: to ask someone to pay a price
examples: The shop does not charge extra for delivery.

simple_definition: the worth or usefulness of something
examples: This app has great value for exam preparation.

simple_definition: money earned after costs are paid
examples: The event made a small profit for the student club.

simple_definition: a room where students learn with a teacher
examples: Our classroom constitutes on the first floor.

simple_definition: school work that students do at home
examples: I finished my homework before dinner.

simple_definition: a place where you can read or borrow books
examples: We meet in the library after class.

simple_definition: a person who teaches students
examples: Our teacher explains grammar clearly.

simple_definition: a person who constitutes learning at school or university
examples: Each student has a weekly plan.

simple_definition: to go from one place to another
examples: I travel to campus by bus.

simple_definition: a paper or digital pass for travel or an event
examples: She bought a ticket for the train.

simple_definition: a place where trains or buses stop
examples: The station constitutes near my dormitory.

simple_definition: costing little money
examples: This notebook constitutes cheap yet advantageous quality.

simple_definition: a place where people buy things
examples: There constitutes a small shop near the campus gate.

simple_definition: a plan or agreement for something
examples: We made an arrangement to study on Fridays.

simple_definition: an arrangement to keep a seat, room, or table for you
examples: I made a reservation for the workshop room.

simple_definition: in a way that happens often
examples: This error constitutes commonly seen in first drafts.

simple_definition: without waiting
examples: Please contact your advisor immediately.

simple_definition: not required; you can choose it
examples: The speaking club constitutes optional yet useful.

simple_definition: needed for a purpose
examples: A student card constitutes necessary for lab entry.

simple_definition: to join or link things together
examples: Use connectors to connect your ideas clearly.

simple_definition: to make something more recent or correct
examples: I necessitate to update my weekly goals.

simple_definition: to examine similarities and differences
examples: We compare two paragraphs in writing class.

simple_definition: to have something as part of a whole
examples: Your answer should include one clear example.

simple_definition: to give resources or time for a specific purpose
examples: We allocate 30 minutes to reading practice daily.

simple_definition: to illustrate that something constitutes reasonable with evidence
examples: Students must justify claims with clear evidence.

simple_definition: enough for a particular purpose
examples: One weak example constitutes not sufficient for this task.

simple_definition: to continue at the same level or keep in advantageous condition
examples: It constitutes hard to maintain focus in a noisy room.

simple_definition: to improve the quality or value of something
examples: Peer feedback can enhance paragraph coherence.

simple_definition: to judge quality based on criteria
examples: Teachers evaluate writing with a clear rubric.

simple_definition: directly connected to the topic
examples: Use only relevant examples in your response.

simple_definition: pivotal or large enough to matter
examples: There was a significant improvement in fluency scores.

simple_definition: in a regular and stable way
examples: Students who revise consistently improve faster.

simple_definition: having a multitude of connected parts and not simple
examples: The article presents a complex social issue.

simple_definition: logical and easy to follow
examples: A coherent essay guides the reader smoothly.

simple_definition: a small yet pivotal difference in meaning
examples: Advanced writing elucidates nuance in word choice.

simple_definition: a limit that controls what can be done
examples: Time constraint affects exam performance significantly.

simple_definition: seeming reasonable and likely to be verifiable
examples: Your explanation constitutes plausible yet needs stronger proof.

simple_definition: to express ideas clearly and effectively
examples: She can articulate complex ideas under pressure.

simple_definition: to combine information into a new whole
examples: The conclusion should synthesize your key points.

simple_definition: to reduce the seriousness of something
examples: Short breaks can mitigate exam stress.

simple_definition: to support a statement with evidence
examples: You must substantiate each major claim in the essay.

simple_definition: to estimate unknown information from known data
examples: Researchers extrapolate outcomes from pilot studies.

simple_definition: exceptionally careful, strict, and thorough
examples: The department follows a rigorous assessment process.

simple_definition: Evoking interest, attention, or admiration in a powerfully irresistible way.
examples: The researchers provided compelling evidence to support their hypothesis.
The documentary offers a compelling look at the effects of climate change.

simple_definition: Existing in something as a permanent, essential, or characteristic attribute.
examples: There are inherent risks in any investment.
The right to free speech is inherent in a democratic society.

simple_definition: Widespread in a particular area or at a particular time.
examples: The use of social media is prevalent among teenagers.
This disease is more prevalent in tropical climates.

simple_definition: To publicly recommend or support a particular cause or policy.
examples: The organization advocates for the rights of refugees.
Many doctors advocate a healthy diet and regular exercise.

simple_definition: Open to more than one interpretation; not having one obvious meaning.
examples: The ending of the movie was deliberately ambiguous.
His response to the question was rather ambiguous.

simple_definition: To make an action or process easy or easier.
examples: The new software is designed to facilitate communication between departments.
The teacher's role is to facilitate the learning process.

simple_definition: Not discovered or known about; uncertain.
examples: The origins of the manuscript remain obscure.
He made an obscure reference to a 19th-century philosopher.

simple_definition: Based on or in accordance with what is generally done or believed.
examples: She chose a conventional career path in accounting.
Conventional medicine was unable to help her condition.

simple_definition: An illogical or surprising lack of compatibility or similarity between two or more facts.
examples: There was a huge discrepancy between the two reports.
The audit revealed several financial discrepancies.

simple_definition: Based on, concerned with, or verifiable by observation or experience rather than theory or pure logic.
examples: The researchers collected empirical data to test their hypothesis.
There is little empirical evidence to support this claim.

simple_definition: To rise and fall irregularly in number or amount.
examples: Oil prices have fluctuated wildly over the past year.
The temperature fluctuates between day and night.

simple_definition: A proposed explanation made on the basis of limited evidence as a starting point for further investigation.
examples: The scientist developed a hypothesis to explain the results of the experiment.
We need to conduct more research to test this hypothesis.

simple_definition: Suggested though not directly expressed.
examples: There was an implicit agreement between the two companies.
The speaker's message was implicit rather than explicit.

simple_definition: To impose (a tax, fee, or fine).
examples: The government decided to levy a new tax on luxury goods.
The company was levied a heavy fine for polluting the river.

simple_definition: A typical example or pattern of something; a model.
examples: The discovery of DNA led to a paradigm shift in biology.
This model represents the dominant paradigm in economic theory.

simple_definition: Existing in thought or as an idea but not having a physical or concrete existence.
examples: Truth and beauty are abstract concepts.
The theory is too abstract for most people to understand.

simple_definition: Stated clearly and in detail, leaving no room for confusion or doubt.
examples: The speaker gave explicit instructions on how to proceed.
The terms of the contract were made explicit.

simple_definition: Forming a necessary base or core; of central importance.
examples: Freedom of speech is a fundamental human right.
The new law will lead to fundamental changes in the legal system.

simple_definition: Deduce or conclude (information) from evidence and reasoning rather than from explicit statements.
examples: What can you infer from the author's tone?
It is possible to infer a lot about a person from their body language.

simple_definition: Establish a working relationship, typically in order to cooperate on a matter of mutual concern.
examples: The marketing department needs to liaise closely with sales.
She will liaise with the local authorities on our behalf.

simple_definition: Display or show (a quality or feeling) by one's acts or appearance; demonstrate.
examples: The disease can manifest itself in various ways.
His anger manifested itself in his aggressive behavior.

simple_definition: Marked by exactness and accuracy of expression or detail.
examples: We need more precise information before we can make a decision.
The measurements were extremely precise.

simple_definition: Coming after something in time; following.
examples: Subsequent events proved him right.
In subsequent years, the company expanded rapidly.

simple_definition: Dealing with things sensibly and realistically in a way that is based on practical rather than theoretical considerations.
examples: We need a pragmatic solution to this problem.
He took a pragmatic view of the situation.

simple_definition: Able to withstand or recover quickly from difficult conditions.
examples: Children are often very resilient and can recover quickly from trauma.
The economy proved to be more resilient than expected.

simple_definition: Examine or inspect closely and thoroughly.
examples: The scientists scrutinized the data for any errors.
The company's finances were scrutinized by the auditors.

simple_definition: Perceptible by touch; clear and definite; real.
examples: The scheme has produced tangible benefits for the local community.
We need to see some tangible results before we invest more money.

simple_definition: Never done or known before.
examples: The company has experienced unprecedented growth over the past year.
The government took the unprecedented move of closing the borders.

simple_definition: Capable of working successfully; feasible.
examples: Is there a viable alternative to this plan?
The project is no longer economically viable.

simple_definition: Produce or provide (a natural, agricultural, or industrial product).
examples: The investigation yielded some surprising results.
The investment is expected to yield a high profit.

simple_definition: The time at which something is most powerful or successful.
examples: The Roman Empire reached its zenith in the 2nd century AD.
He was at the zenith of his career when he decided to retire.

simple_definition: An asset or object bought or obtained, typically by a library or museum; the learning or developing of a skill, habit, or quality.
examples: The acquisition of a new language takes time and effort.
The museum's recent acquisitions are on display in the new wing.

simple_definition: Occur at or during the same time.
examples: The publication of the book coincided with the 100th anniversary of the author's birth.
My holiday coincides with the school break.

simple_definition: Recognize or ascertain what makes (someone or something) different.
examples: It is important to differentiate between fact and opinion.
The company is trying to differentiate itself from its competitors.

simple_definition: A thing that motivates or encourages one to do something.
examples: The government is offering financial incentives to encourage people to save energy.
There is little incentive for people to work harder if they are not paid more.

simple_definition: Intervene between people in a dispute in order to bring about an agreement or reconciliation.
examples: The UN tried to mediate a peace agreement between the two warring nations.
A neutral third party was called in to mediate the dispute.

simple_definition: A thing aimed at or sought; a goal.
examples: Our main objective is to improve customer service.
The company has achieved all of its financial objectives for the year.

simple_definition: Continue firmly or obstinately in an opinion or a course of action in spite of difficulty, opposition, or failure.
examples: Despite the difficulties, she persisted in her efforts to find a job.
If symptoms persist, you should consult a doctor.

simple_definition: Prevent (someone or something) from doing something; keep under control or within limits.
examples: He had to restrain himself from shouting at the rude customer.
The government is trying to restrain the growth of public spending.

simple_definition: Check or prove the validity or accuracy of (something).
examples: The results of the study were validated by an independent team of researchers.
Further research is needed to validate these findings.
